title: orchis mascula subsp. mascula
intro: Orchis mascula subsp. mascula, also known as the early purple orchid or military orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family, native to Europe and western Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include a compound called magnesium nonotide, an inhibitor of phosphodiesterase enzymes. The health benefits of Orchis mascula subsp. mascula include its potential to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to relieve edema and other related symptoms. Herbal preparations of Orchis mascula subsp. mascula include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
